description: Relying on setAccessible reflection into JDK internals that strong encapsulation (JPMS) blocks at runtime on newer JDKs.
body: Failure mode: code uses reflection with setAccessible(true) to reach private or JDK-internal members; on Java 9+ with module encapsulation this throws InaccessibleObjectException, and a library doing so breaks after a JDK upgrade. Detection signals: InaccessibleObjectException or 'module does not export' at runtime; works on JDK 8 but fails on 17+; warnings about illegal reflective access. Do not reflect into encapsulated internals as a supported mechanism. Safer fallback: use the public/supported API, request access via --add-opens only as a deliberate temporary measure, or upgrade the offending library; verify on the target JDK version, not just the old one.
